candidates.stmt:
I’m standing for election as a Liberal Democrat because my belief in democracy is personal, not abstract. Having grown up under a communist system, I understand what it means when voices are silenced—and why it matters that every voice here in West Lancashire is heard, respected, and represented.

I’ve lived and worked in this community since 2003. Over that time, I’ve seen how West Lancashire has changed—economically, socially, and demographically—and I know that the solutions we need must reflect the realities on the ground. I believe in strong local government: decisions made closer to the people they affect, with real accountability and local knowledge at their heart.

We face clear challenges. Housing must be affordable, but also sustainable, protecting our environment while supporting the traditional industries that define our area. Issues like flooding and sewage in our waterways aren’t just national concerns; they affect our fields, our homes, and our health right here. These are priorities I will fight to address.

As a parent with two children still in school, I see every day how vital well-funded, high-quality education is for our future. Living in a rural area, I also know how limited public transport and poor infrastructure can hold people back—from accessing jobs, education, and services.

My commitment to our local healthcare services is deeply personal. My daughter spent three months in the neonatal unit at Ormskirk Hospital, and like so many families, we have relied on emergency services in times of need. These are not abstract institutions—they are lifelines, and they must be protected and strengthened.

I want West Lancashire to be a place where families can thrive—where we protect our environment, support local industries, invest in our public services, and build a fairer, more resilient community for everyone.

I’ve lived in West Lancs most of my life and have with my wife raised our family here. I am passionate about protecting the environment and will be an advocate for all ecological issues, and work with the community and local businesses to help raise awareness of the things we can change to protect the environment.

I bring 8 years experience of being a Councillor, with 3 years leading several Cabinet portfolios.

I enjoy travelling, reading, film and theatre. My main interest is music, I have worked in production and management, as well as a volunteer on hospital radio.

I am a local man, passionate about our local area and local people, that’s why I am a Parish Councillor.
I will fight passionately for our area, ensuring issues such as the crumbling roads and flooding are addressed and ensure that our green belt land is protected.
I am a HCPC registered Paramedic and work in the NHS as an Emergency Care Practitioner. I live in Up Holland with my wife, our children and dog. In my spare time I enjoy working on and using classic vehicles, holidays and listening to music. I am originally from Yorkshire, but moved over to Lancashire about ten years ago, Up Holland is the only place that feels like home.

Simon Evans: The Local Voice for West Lancashire:

I have worked within the Criminal Justice System for 30 years specialising in community justice and anti-social behaviour. As a father of four children, I believe in standards throughout our society, standards which are being decayed throughout our communities. Living in West Lancashire for 20 years, it’s a county I call home and I am determined to keep it as beautiful and thriving as it can be for all people to enjoy.

I have implemented New Problem-solving courts for the UK Governments, Restorative Justice programmes across the globe, youth services provisions, innovative prison services and works closely with Victims of Crime and Anti-Social Behaviour through the UK, in the courts and in the wider community.

Together let’s Make Britain Great!

To do this, reform is essential in the way our country is run and managed, so it works properly for the people. In many areas, just the application of basic common sense would be a good start!

The nation faces many challenges, but we can overcome them. Let’s celebrate our pride in being British: our amazing culture, our incredible heritage. Let’s stop all the woke nonsense that is holding us back. Let’s have a proper immigration policy that works for our country and protects our borders.

Personal Freedoms:

Our freedoms as individuals are curtailed daily by more and more legislation that instructs people what they can or cannot say, The days of an Orwellian state has truly arrived, in the guise of a Woke doctrine designed to frighten citizens into submission of compliance to this narrow view of life or other people’s view of how life should be conducted, in a twisted morality, and without your compliance you will be cancelled and branded a social pariah.

By Voting for Reform and Reform only, you can guarantee the freedom of speech and freedom of thought not only for you but for your children and future generations.

Delivery of Politics

Independent candidate for Ormskirk West under the Our West Lancashire group. My focus is local issues that matter to residents, and with no party whip, I can vote freely to give residents a voice in their community. I’m local, approachable and willing to put the work in to make Ormskirk an even better place.

Gareth has spent his entire adult life living and working in West Lancashire, volunteering within the local community and campaigning for improvements to residents’ lives.

Gareth has lived and worked in Ormskirk since 2008 and has been a Borough Councillor here since 2014. He has spent his life working and volunteering to support others, through his union, Labour, and co-operative work; for Alzheimer’s Society; serving in community roles including, school governor, on Ormskirk School Foundation Trust, Ormskirk School Local Academy Council and rugby club Director.

He also supports our local Credit Union where he is Vice Chair, South West Lancs Independent Community Advice Network (ICAN), and is a member of West Lancashire’s community renewable energy group – setup to reduce energy costs and invest in our community.

Passionate about Ormskirk, he is responsible for securing speedbumps on Cottage Lane, saving the red phone box in the town and widespread investment in local facilities from parks and playgrounds to town centre heritage and local healthcare settings.

Gareth says:
_“Over the past 12 years, I have worked closely with residents, community groups, fellow councillors, council officers and other organisations to bring about improvements to the lives of the people of Ormskirk.

“Through small interventions and partnership working, we have been able to bring about successes such as road resurfacing, infrastructure investment and town centre activities.

“I have lived in Ormskirk all my adult life and I have previously worked for Alzheimer’s Society, working in partnership with local agencies to set up Dementia Friendly West Lancashire in 2016.

“In the ward I have chaired the Police and Communities Together (PACT) meetings for many years, until police funding cuts abolished them, but still work closely with Lancashire Constabulary.

“And we continue to keep the developers at bay, protecting our green space and putting a stop to further student House in Multipl

George is a long-time champion of making resident voices heard. An experienced councillor, George is passionate about the area and wants to help young families to create better opportunities for our young people. He has been a strong voice on planning and environmental issues. He wants to ensure that residents’ concerns are addressed seriously by working closely with residents, the Parish Council, Police and Voluntary Community Faith Sector groups to strengthen the Scarisbrick community and deliver much needed services.

I am standing for Labour because I believe in fairness, opportunity and communities where everyone can live safely and thrive. 
As a retired primary headteacher, a Christian and a governor at a local Church primary school, I have spent my life serving young people and supporting families. 
I believe in protecting our NHS, supporting  local schools and ensuring people feel safe and valued in their neighbourhoods. 
Labour values of social cohesion, fairness and respect for people of all backgrounds are the foundations of a peaceful and thriving community. 
I want to work for residents to protect our countryside, opposing fracking on prime agriculture land and in or near housing, improve local roads and flood prevention, support affordable local transport and promote clean, sustainable energy, for the future of Rural West ward.

Council tax increase Reform promise change. They deliver worse services . LCC increase - 3.8% . Labour led WLBC delivered balanced budget and 2.99% increase.

**Labour Delivering a Balanced Budget for West Lancashire**
Labour has agreed a balanced £19.538 million budget for 2026/27, putting the council’s finances on a stronger footing than last year and supporting long-term planning with a £1.1 million increase in core spending power from government. The budget protects key services residents rely on. Leisure centres are protected, fortnightly bin collections continue with additional staff support, and street cleaning and litter bins will be maintained across the borough. Investment will also continue in parks and green spaces. The council is also increasing Council Tax Support up to 100% for the most vulnerable residents, alongside a £100,000 fund for local charities and voluntary organisations. Changes to car parking in Ormskirk will help support visitors, local businesses and the town centre economy.

I grew up in Skelmersdale where I still live and work. Through my work in adult education I knows how important it is that individuals, families and communities receive the right support to help them achieve positive futures.

I’m a trade union representative and am committed to fighting inequality, particularly child poverty.

I am very active in the community and am involved with the Right to Food Campaign and Football Fans Supporting Foodbanks locally.

As a firm supporter of the arts, I’m also passionate about the environment and am a member of Skelmersdale’s Ramblers group.

I have represented Skelmersdale South since 2018 and am now the Portfolio Holder for Health and Wellbeing on West Lancashire Borough Council.

Over the last four years, I’ve played an active role as a ward councillor, overseeing improvements to local green spaces, working with police to tackle anti-social behaviour, improving car parking and working closely with the CCG to tackle health inequalities for residents within West Lancashire.

I work for the Children and Family Wellbeing Service and am also the West Lancashire lead for supporting victims of domestic abuse.

I have been the manager of the Premier convenience store on Dingle Road for over 12 years, building the business up from an empty shop unit.

Having previously served as an Up Holland Parish Councillor for several years, I believe in working together to support our local communities.

During the pandemic, I set up a collection point for customers to support the local food bank and have helped to raise funds for people in need.

As the North West District President of the National Federation of Independent Retailers, I am also keen to get more support for local businesses.

I worked as a lecturer at West Lancashire College from 2009 to 2015 and I have worked training the next generation of teachers at Edge Hill University since 2015. I have taught hundreds of former students who now live and work around the council area. In 2010 I stood as the Green Party Parliamentary candidate for West Lancashire and again in the Parliamentary by-election in 2023.

As someone who has been a Green member for over 25 years, I've fought for social justice as an anti-racism campaigner on Merseyside, worked alongside anti-fracking campaigns and campaigning for a fairer society. As a father of three, my teenage children face a challenging world. When I was born CO2 was 327 parts per million (ppm) in the atmosphere - it is now at 429 ppm and rising - the climate crisis has never been more urgent.

There are those who deny science, stoke division in society and blame the poorest and the most vulnerable. There are politicians who are funded and influenced by the millionaires, billionaires and those who hold power in society, and bend to what they want. The Greens stand opposed to that.

We stand to represent local people and listen to concerns, for lower bills, for cleaner cheaper energy and to make hope normal again.
